_note: Updated by Animation after each task. Reports to Development. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Define the animation state machine for the player character — all movement and combat states
priorities.note: Blocked on game systems locking the movement and combat parameters. The animation state machine must match the game's mechanical state — states that don't exist in the game are wasted animation work.
priorities.proposed_solution: After movement and combat numbers lock, specify the full player animation state machine so frames match real game states.

priorities.title: Finalize the spritesheet JSON export format and lock it before any game runtime work begins
priorities.note: The game runtime will consume the spritesheet export format. Any format change after the runtime starts consuming it is a breaking change. This needs to be stable before engine integration starts.
priorities.proposed_solution: Finalize the spritesheet JSON export contract with Development, version it, and freeze it before runtime starts consuming it.

priorities.title: Establish pixel art quality standards — palette rules, silhouette requirements, anti-aliasing policy
priorities.note: Production gate published at `docs/pixel-art-quality-standards.md`: palette (AH-* + craft rules), silhouette (bible §3–6 + animation bar), anti-aliasing (opaque-or-transparent, lossless, pixelated preview). §5 checklist aligns with charter `spritesheet-audit`. Future work: optional linter/export warnings in the workbench — not blocking this milestone.
priorities.proposed_solution: Treat the doc as canonical for reviews; run `spritesheet-audit` against §5 for each submission. Revise the doc when art bible v0.3 locks new tokens or biome rules.

risks_p1.title: Spritesheet export format not yet locked
risks_p1.note: Game runtime work cannot begin until this is stable. Any format change after runtime integration starts is a breaking change.

actions.name: Animation Spec
actions.description: Complete animation spec — frame count, pixel dimensions, state machine, timing parameters, export format.
actions.trigger: A new character, ability, or effect is approved for production

actions.name: Spritesheet Audit
actions.description: Quality assessment — palette compliance, silhouette clarity, anti-aliasing compliance, frame consistency.
actions.trigger: Any submitted sprite or animation asset

actions.name: AI Pipeline Recommendation
actions.description: Model or LoRA recommendation, prompt architecture, and validation criteria for AI-assisted animation production.
actions.trigger: AI-assisted animation workflow is requested for a new asset type

opportunities.title: Evaluate AI animation tools (EbSynth, AnimateDiff) for the Ashen Hollow production pipeline
opportunities.note: Pixel art quality standards are published (`docs/pixel-art-quality-standards.md`). Evaluation can proceed against §5 checklist and art bible gates.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Evaluate EbSynth, AnimateDiff, or alternatives against the published bars; recommend a production workflow and validation steps for Human-in-the-loop cleanup.
